summary: The user session involved an attempt to proceed to checkout, which was interrupted by a non-functional '101' button (dead click). This led to a navigation U-turn back to the products page. On the product details page, the user successfully added an item to the cart. The session is characterized by good core web vitals for the main content but is marred by a significant UX issue (dead click and U-turn) and several network inefficiencies, including failed third-party ad pings and numerous redundant calls to a Google consent service on each page load.

issues.title: Dead Click on '101' Button
issues.description: The user clicked a button labeled '101' on the cart page, likely intended for quantity adjustment or a similar interaction, but no discernible action or network request followed. This indicates a non-responsive element, leading to user frustration and a navigation U-turn.

evidence.detail: Selector: #product-1 > td:nth-of-type(4) > button, Text: 101, Page: https://automationexercise.com/view_cart

impact.description: The user spent 3.075 seconds attempting to interact with a non-functional element, leading to confusion and a change in their intended flow.

issues.title: Navigation U-turn after Checkout Attempt
issues.description: After attempting to proceed to checkout and encountering a dead click, the user abandoned the checkout flow and navigated back to the products page. This U-turn suggests frustration or a roadblock in the checkout process.

impact.description: The user wasted time and effort by navigating away from their initial goal (checkout) due to a perceived issue.

issues.title: Failed Third-Party Ad Pings
issues.description: Two POST requests to 'pagead2.googlesyndication.com/pagead/ping?e=1' failed with a status of 0. This typically indicates a network error, a blocked request (e.g., by an ad blocker), or an issue with the ad service itself.

impact.description: While not directly impacting user experience (response body is 0 bytes), failed ad pings can indicate issues with monetization or analytics tracking, and consume client-side resources.

issues.title: Excessive Redundant Third-Party Calls
issues.description: Multiple identical POST requests to 'fundingchoicesmessages.google.com' were made within very short timeframes on both page loads. There were 8 such calls on the /products page and another 8 on the /product_details/2 page. While these requests returned 204 (No Content) quickly, their sheer number suggests potential inefficiency in third-party script loading or consent management.

impact.description: These requests add unnecessary network overhead and client-side processing, potentially delaying other critical resources, even if individual durations are low. The request bodies are also non-trivial in size.

issues.title: Incomplete Web Vitals Reporting
issues.description: The 'domInteractive' and 'domComplete' web vital metrics are reported as 0, which is unusual and suggests an issue with how these metrics are being captured or reported in the session data.

impact.description: This issue primarily affects the ability to accurately monitor and debug page performance, rather than directly impacting the user experience in this session.

networkReport.summary: The session exhibited a moderate network health. Two third-party ad pings failed with a status of 0. A significant number of redundant POST requests (16 in total) were made to Google's funding choices messages service across two page loads, indicating potential inefficiency. Main application requests were generally fast, with the 'add to cart' operation completing in 257ms.

redundantCalls.description: Multiple identical calls made within ~1 second on the /products page.
Multiple identical calls made within ~1 second on the /product_details/2 page.

largePayloads.description: Configuration data for ad traffic quality.
Configuration data for ad traffic quality.
Cloudflare RUM beacon with a large request body containing performance metrics.
Cloudflare RUM beacon with a large request body containing performance metrics.

recommendations.action: Investigate and resolve failed third-party ad pings.
recommendations.codeExample: Check ad blocker interference, network connectivity, or ad script configuration. Ensure ad scripts are loaded correctly and not blocked by CSP.

recommendations.action: Optimize third-party script loading for Google Funding Choices messages.
recommendations.codeExample: Review the integration of the Google Funding Choices script. Ensure it's loaded only once per page or debounced/throttled if multiple calls are triggered by client-side events. Consider lazy loading or deferring non-critical third-party scripts.

uxReport.summary: The user experience was negatively impacted by a dead click on a critical path (checkout), leading to a navigation U-turn. This indicates a significant point of friction and potential loss of conversion. Otherwise, the 'Add to cart' functionality worked as expected.

deadClicks.description: User clicked a button that did not trigger any visible change or network request.

navigationUturns.description: User initiated checkout, then navigated away from the cart page after encountering a non-responsive element.

frustrationEvents.description: User abandoned checkout flow to navigate to products page.

recommendations.action: Ensure all interactive elements, especially in critical flows like checkout, are functional.
recommendations.codeExample: Investigate the JavaScript associated with the '#product-1 > td:nth-of-type(4) > button' element. Ensure event listeners are correctly attached and not failing. Use browser developer tools to debug: `document.querySelector('#product-1 > td:nth-of-type(4) > button').addEventListener('click', function(e) { console.log('Button clicked!'); /* Add your logic here */ });`

recommendations.action: Review the checkout flow for potential roadblocks or confusing interactions.
recommendations.codeExample: Conduct user testing on the checkout process. Implement analytics to track abandonment rates at each step. Consider clearer visual feedback for user actions.

recommendations.action: Fix the dead click on the '101' button in the cart.
recommendations.codeExample: Examine the HTML and JavaScript for the button with selector `#product-1 > td:nth-of-type(4) > button` on `https://automationexercise.com/view_cart`. Ensure that the button has an active event listener and that its associated function executes correctly to update quantity or perform another expected action. Check for JavaScript errors in the console when the button is clicked.

recommendations.action: Investigate and resolve failed third-party ad pings.
recommendations.codeExample: Check server-side logs for `pagead2.googlesyndication.com` requests. Verify if ad blockers are widely used by your audience. Ensure Content Security Policy (CSP) allows these requests if implemented. If using a tag manager, verify the tag configuration.

recommendations.action: Optimize third-party script loading for Google Funding Choices messages.
Address incomplete web vitals reporting for `domInteractive` and `domComplete`.
recommendations.codeExample: Verify the configuration of your web vitals monitoring tool. Ensure it's correctly capturing and reporting all standard metrics. This might involve checking the SDK version or integration method. If custom reporting, ensure the correct browser APIs are being used: `performance.timing.domInteractive` and `performance.timing.domComplete`.
